{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"I need to find polygon/shapefile/GeoJSON data for SMALL TERRITORIES and ISLANDS that may not be in major datasets. 33→|-------|---------|---------|\n    34→| whep_equivalences | 1226 rows, 179 unique polities | FT + CShapes + COW merged |\n    35→| COWs full entities changes | 381 | Territorial transfers with Historical polities database (via colleague's Excel)\n    48→- **UN M49**: United Nations Standard Country Codes\n    49→- **FAOSTAT**: FAO country definitions\n    50→- **CIA World Factbook** (area references in colleague's notes)\n    51→\n    52→## 3. Methodology\n    53→\n    54→### 3.1 Definition of \"Polity\"\n    55→Per WHEP project: A polity is a **fixed territory over a continuous period of time**.\n    56→- Territory change = new polity (even if same country name)\n    57→- Name change only (no territory change) = same polity\n    58→- Most colonies tracked separately if they have individual trade data\n    59→\n    60→### 3.2 Polity Code Format\n    61→`XXX-yyyy-YYYY` where:\n    62→- `XXX` = ISO3 code or artificial code\n    63→- `yyyy` = start year\n    64→- `YYYY` = end year\n    65→\n    66→### 3.3 Verification Process\n    67→1. Parse all 603 polity codes and extract year ranges\n    68→2. Group by ISO prefix to detect overlaps/gaps\n    69→3. Cross-reference each era (1860-1920, 1920-1945, 1945-1975, 1975-1991, 1991-2025)\n    70→   against COW territorial changes, CShapes boundaries, and Cliopatria records\n    71→4. Compare with colleague's whep_equivalences working sheet\n    72→5. Flag discrepancies with severity levels:\n    73→   - **CRITICAL**: Wrong dates affecting data joins\n    74→   - **IMPORTANT**: Missing polity periods for significant territorial changes\n    75→   - **MINOR**: Design choices that could be revisited\n    76→   - **NOTE**: Observations for documentation\n    77→\n    78→### 3.4 Area Threshold\n    79→Per colleague's delimitations sheet:\n    80→- Territorial change >= 10% of total polity area = new polity boundary"}]},"uuid":"d0104f5f-0690-44c4-83ba-084572dff598","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:06:57.025Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"0cfa64cd-8502-4c53-8d36-de56ab89b01f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Code Prefix Collisions\n     4→\n     5→Multiple unrelated polities share the same 3-letter prefix. This is NOT a bug\n     6→(the full polity_code is unique), but can cause confusion when filtering by prefix.\n     7→\n     8→| Prefix | Polities | Issue |\n     9→|--------|----------|-------|\n    10→| BEL | Belgium (BEL-1831-2025), Belgian Congo (BEL-1885-2025) | Same colonizer code |\n    11→| CAN | Canary Islands, Canton/Enderbury Is., Canada (x2) | Unrelated entities |\n    12→| DAN | Danish India, Danish Virgin Islands | Same colonizer code |\n    13→| DUT | Dutch West Indies, Dutch New Guinea | Same colonizer code |\n    14→| GER | Germany/Zollverein, German colonies Oceania | Same country code |\n    15→| NET | Netherlands (old), Netherlands Antilles (original) | Related entities |\n    16→| ORA | Orange Free State, Orange Free State (to 1910) | POTENTIAL DUPLICATE |\n    17→| PAL | Palmyra Island, Palestine/Jordan | Unrelated entities |\n    18→| PAN | Panama (before Canal Zone), Panama | Related (same country) |\n    19→| PRI | Prince Edward Island, Puerto Rico | Unrelated entities |\n    20→| SAR | Sardinia, Sarawak | Unrelated entities |\n    21→| SPA | Spanish North Africa, Spanish Guinea | Same colonizer code |\n    22→| ST. | St. Helena, St. Lucia, St. Vincent | Different islands |\n    23→| SUD | Sudan (Anglo-Egyptian), Sudan (1956-2011) | Related (same country) |\n    24→| SWE | Sweden, Sweden (to 1905) | POTENTIAL DUPLICATE (see below) |\n    25→| SYR | Syria and Lebanon, Syrian Arab Republic (x2) | Related entities |\n    26→| TIB | Tibet (old), Tibet (1913-1914), Tibet (1914-1950) | Related entities |\n    27→| VAN | Van Diemen's Land, Vancouver's Island | Unrelated entities |\n    28→\n    29→**Recommendation**: No action needed. The full polity_code (XXX-yyyy-YYYY) is\n    30→always unique. Document this in the polities documentation.\n    31→\n    32→## 2. Potential Duplicate Entries\n    33→\n    34→### 2.1 \"Kingdom Two sicilies\" vs \"Two Sicilies\"\n    35→- KIN-1800-1860: \"Kingdom Two sicilies\" (from Federico-Tena?)\n    36→- TWO-1800-1860: \"Two Sicilies\" (from CShapes?)\n    37→\n    38→**Investigation needed**: Check common_names.csv to see if these map to different\n    39→source entries or if one should be removed. They cover the exact same territory\n    40→and time period.\n    41→\n    42→### 2.2 \"Sweden\" vs \"Sweden (to 1905)\"\n    43→- SWE-1800-2025: Appears to be an aggregate FT entity\n    44→- SWE-1800-1905: CShapes-based pre-Norway-independence Sweden\n    45→\n    46→**Investigation needed**: If SWE-1800-2025 is a FAOSTAT aggregate, this is by\n    47→design. But verify these don't both feed into the same polity after aggregation.\n    48→\n    49→### 2.3 \"Orange Free State\" vs \"Orange Free State (to 1910)\"\n    50→- ORA-1848-1900: From FT (ends at Boer War)\n    51→- ORA-1800-1910: Broader period including pre-independence and annexation to SA\n    52→\n    53→**Investigation needed**: These overlap significantly.\n    54→\n    55→### 2.4 \"Ethiopia (old)\" vs Ethiopia periods\n    56→- ETH-1800-2025: FAOSTAT aggregate entity\n    57→- ETH-1800-1889 through ETH-1993-2025: CShapes-based periods\n    58→\n    59→Same pattern as other \"old\" aggregate entities. By design.\n    60→\n    61→## 3. Timeline Gaps (Intentional vs Problematic)\n    62→\n    63→### 3.1 Intentional Gaps (polity absorbed into larger entity)\n    64→| Polity | Gap | Explanation |\n    65→|--------|-----|-------------|\n    66→| Estonia | 1940-1991 | Absorbed into USSR |\n    67→| Latvia | 1940-1991 | Absorbed into USSR |\n    68→| Lithuania | 1940-1991 | Absorbed into USSR |\n    69→| Montenegro | 1915-2006 | Absorbed into Yugoslavia/Serbia |\n    70→| Serbia | 1915-2006 | Absorbed into Yugoslavia |\n    71→| Germany | 1945-1990 | Split into FRG/GDR |\n    72→| Vietnam | 1893-1954 | Under French Indochina, then split |\n    73→| Mayotte | 1914-2002 | Absorbed into Madagascar, then France |\n    74→\n    75→These are all correct - the \"missing\" polity periods are covered by the\n    76→parent/absorbing entity.\n    77→\n    78→### 3.2 Gap Requiring Attention\n    79→| Polity | Gap | Issue |\n    80→|--------|-----|-------|"}]},"uuid":"d5ab97e3-f0f0-407a-b74b-e5831543e1f4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:06:58.160Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2b1dd449-46de-4485-af22-5ac2f9d8bfc8","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Version 5 will be released in January 2026.\n\n## Small Territories and Islands\n\nGADM's coverage includes numerous small territories and islands. The database includes entries for Falkland Islands, Faroe Islands, Fiji, French Polynesia, French Southern Territories, Grenada, Guadeloupe, Guam, Marshall Islands, Martinique, Montserrat, New Caledonia, Niue, Norfolk Island, Northern Mariana Islands, Pitcairn Islands, Saint-Barthélemy, Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha, Saint Pierre and Miquelon, Samoa, Seychelles, Solomon Islands, South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands, Tokelau, Tonga, Turks and Caicos Islands, Tuvalu, United States Minor Outlying Islands, and Virgin Islands.\n\n## Available Formats\n\nAfter selecting a country, users will see options to download the data in different formats: GeoPackage, Shapefile, or GeoJSON. Free vector and raster map data at 1:10m, 1:50m, and 1:110m scales\",\"url\":\"https://www.naturalearthdata.com/downloads/10m-cultural-vectors/10m-admin-0-countries/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I can provide you with information about Natural Earth's coverage of small territories and islands:\n\n## Coverage of Small Territories and Island Nations\n\nNatural Earth provides internal boundaries and polygons for all but a few tiny island nations. This means that while most small territories are included, there are some exceptions for extremely small islands.\n\n## Administrative Data Structure\n\nNatural Earth includes matched boundary lines and polygons with names attributes for countries and sovereign states, including dependencies (French Polynesia), map units (U.S. Pacific Island Territories) and sub-national map subunits (Corsica versus mainland Metropolitan France).\n\n## Island Coverage Details\n\nFor islands specifically, Natural Earth provides additional small ocean islands ranked to two levels of relative importance. However, ocean bathymetry and ocean polygon are not punched out for minor islands, nor are they included in land polygon or any country or state (admin-0 and admin-1) polygons.\n\n## Boundary Representation\n\nNatural Earth Vector draws boundaries of sovereign states according to defacto status, meaning the dataset represents territories based on who actually controls them rather than legal claims.\n\nThe search results don't specifically mention the coverage status of Andorra, Monaco, Vatican, or Gibraltar individually, but based on the general coverage described, these small territories would typically be included in Natural Earth's country-level (admin-0) polygon datasets.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"461b55ea-7e13-409f-96bc-1b31e6748127","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:07:15.181Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"5f79528b-f06c-487d-ac4b-6f1a3e894348","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"0633e930-8e33-4fc1-be6c-16a330abde29","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PcuisW3ULqWgamLiCqWW4J","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"historical colonial territories shapefile GIS boundaries French Indochina British Malaya Belgian Congo\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Where to Find GIS Data for Historical Country Boundaries - Geography Realm\",\"url\":\"https://www.geographyrealm.com/find-gis-data-historical-country-boundaries/\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"Download data by country | DIVA-GIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.diva-gis.org/gdata\"},{\"title\":\"Atlas of colonialism - Wikimedia Commons\",\"url\":\"https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Atlas_of_colonialism\"},{\"title\":\"(De)Colonial historical geography and historical GIS - ScienceDirect\",\"url\":\"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0305748822000846\"},{\"title\":\"43 Maps of french indochina Images: PICRYL - Public Domain Media Search Engine Public Domain Search\",\"url\":\"https://picryl.com/topics/maps+of+french+indochina\"},{\"title\":\"Category:Maps of French Indochina - Wikimedia Commons\",\"url\":\"https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Maps_of_French_Indochina\"},{\"title\":\"geoBoundaries\",\"url\":\"https://www.geoboundaries.org/\"},{\"title\":\"GIS Boundary Files\",\"url\":\"https://international.ipums.org/international/gis.shtml\"},{\"title\":\"Historical Atlas of the Low Countries: A gis Dataset of Locality-Level Boundaries (1350–1800) - ScienceDirect\",\"url\":\"https://www.sciencedirect.com/org/science/article/pii/S2452366623000622\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here are the key findings about historical colonial territories shapefiles and GIS boundaries:\n\n## Primary Resource for Colonial Territories\n\nThe CShapes 2.0 GIS dataset maps the borders of states and dependent territories from 1886 through 2019, and is no longer limited to independent states, but also maps the borders of colonies and other dependencies, thereby providing near complete global coverage of political units throughout recent history. CShapes 2.0 addresses limitations by backdating borders to 1886 and by mapping colonial dependencies throughout this entire period.\n\n## General Resources for Historical GIS Data\n\nWhile Natural Earth provides free GIS data in shapefile format for current countries at a range of scales, finding historical GIS data is harder to find. Several other options exist:\n\n- Thanks to the Wayback Machine, shapefiles of country boundaries spanning between 2000 BCE and 1994 CE can still be accessed from the now-defunct Thinkquest.org site.\n\n- The HGIS de las Indias site features thousands of boundary files covering colonial Spanish America from 1701 – 1808.\n\n- A Vision of Britain through Time from the University of Portsmouth has made historical boundary information for counties, parishes, and constituencies for England, Wales, and Scotland available in shapefiles format, covering a variety of timeframes in the 1800s and 1900s.\n\nFor the specific colonial territories you mentioned (French Indochina, British Malaya, and Belgian Congo), **CShapes 2.0** appears to be the most comprehensive resource available for accessing shapefile data covering these colonial boundaries.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"99cdb9f9-a3cd-4c21-8596-5da84cd21f07","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:07:17.176Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"0633e930-8e33-4fc1-be6c-16a330abde29","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Here are the key findings:\n\n## Kingdom of the Two Sicilies\n\nThe Kingdom of the Two Sicilies was a kingdom in Southern Italy from 1816 to 1861 under the control of the House of Bourbon-Two Sicilies, and was the largest sovereign state by population and land area in Italy before Italian unification, comprising Sicily and most of southern Italy. The king of the Two Sicilies was overthrown by Giuseppe Garibaldi in 1860, and the annexation of the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies completed the first phase of Italian unification, with the new Kingdom of Italy proclaimed in 1861.\n\n## Papal States\n\nThe Papal States, officially the State of the Church, were a conglomeration of territories on the Italian peninsula under the direct sovereign rule of the pope from 756 to 1870, and were among the major states of Italy from the 8th century until the unification of Italy, which took place between 1859 and 1870. The Papal States occupied a central position in the Italian peninsula, extending roughly between 41°13′N and 45°N latitude, reaching the Po River to the north and bordering the Adriatic coast to the east, with the southern boundary following the Abruzzo mountains.\n\nThe search results provide historical information about these states but do not contain specific information about GIS shapefiles or historical GIS data resources. For accessing all territories at once, you can download the GADM data as a single database in the GeoPackage format (the standard format) or as a geodatabase. However, it is best to download by country if you prefer individual files.\n\n## Licensing Information\n\nThe data are freely available for academic use and other non-commercial use, though redistribution or commercial use is not allowed without prior permission.\n\nYou can access the GADM download pages at gadm.org to download the specific data you need.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"c61dc3b6-0c7e-4056-b2ce-ce7097343255","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:07:27.763Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d6daf64a-c921-40ea-bfd4-6e46b6815ad5","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

martynafford/natural-earth-geojson: Natural Earth data in GeoJSON · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/martynafford/natural-earth-geojson\"}]\n\nI've successfully performed a web search for information about Natural Earth's admin 0 countries list and its coverage of territories, dependencies, and minor islands.\n\nHere are the key findings:\n\n## Overview\nThere are 209 sovereign states in the world, though only 199 issue passports.\n\n## Coverage of Dependencies and Territories\nCountries distinguish between metropolitan (homeland) and independent and semi-independent portions of sovereign states. If you want to see the dependent overseas regions broken out (like in ISO codes, see France for example), use map units instead.\n\nThe dataset includes dependencies (French Polynesia), map units (U.S. Pacific Island Territories) and sub-national map subunits (Corsica versus mainland Metropolitan France).\n\n## Map Units and Subunits\nAuxiliary themes include 298 cartographic map units and 360 subunits optimized for labeling edge cases, like the four constitute countries of England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland in the United Kingdom.\n\n## Boundary Representation\nNatural Earth shows de facto boundaries by default according to who controls the territory, versus de jure. Optional point-of-view (POV) variants are available for several dozen countries.\n\nThe search results provide comprehensive information about how Natural Earth's admin 0 dataset handles countries, territories, dependencies, and minor island coverage across different geographic scales (1:10m, 1:50m, and 1:110m).\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"b026cc11-faed-4c79-aec0-85993b87163a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:07:28.829Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7d2366f5-65e4-4497-91c9-3c74c8ef8e85","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"2e292628-4528-4565-9eb2-95f5030fa473","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01785e9jMPdinRkxyYfLxfDV","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"CShapes 2.0 dataset coverage pre-1886 historical states territories\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"Cshapes 2.0 | Library of Congress\",\"url\":\"https://www.loc.gov/item/2023592015/\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - CShapes\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Research Repository\",\"url\":\"https://repository.essex.ac.uk/31791/\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://ideas.repec.org/a/sae/jocore/v66y2022i1p144-161.html\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - Mapping The International System, 1886-2017: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/publications/cshapes-2/\"},{\"title\":\"CShapes Dataset of Historical Country Boundaries\",\"url\":\"https://demo.ldproxy.net/cshapes\"},{\"title\":\"Comparing CShapes 2.0 with Other GIS Datasets. | Download Scientific Diagram\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/figure/Comparing-CShapes-20-with-Other-GIS-Datasets_tbl1_351339723\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - vLex United States - vLex\",\"url\":\"https://law-journals-books.vlex.com/vid/mapping-the-international-system-879944959\"},{\"title\":\"The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://d-nb.info/125016981X/34\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found regarding CShapes 2.0 dataset coverage:\n\n## Temporal Coverage\n\nCShapes 2.0 is a GIS dataset that maps the borders of states and dependent territories from 1886 through 2019. The dataset does not include pre-1886 historical states and territories in its main coverage.\n\n## What Changed from the Previous Version\n\nThe extension extends temporal coverage from 1946 back to the year 1886, which followed the Berlin Conference on the partition of Africa. The new dataset is no longer limited to independent states, but also maps the borders of colonies and other dependencies, thereby providing near complete global coverage of political units throughout recent history.\n\n## Related Coverage\n\nIt's worth noting that CShapes-Europe extends coverage back to 1816, which is an additional variant of the dataset with earlier temporal coverage for European boundaries.\n\n## Limitations Regarding Earlier Periods\n\nThe current version traces the borders of all units back to 1886 or the onset of colonization, meaning that for pre-1886 periods, the dataset only covers territories from the point colonization began in specific regions.\n\nIn summary, the CShapes 2.0 dataset does not provide comprehensive coverage of pre-1886 historical states and territories on a global scale, though the European variant extends back to 1816.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"29ebbaa6-6136-4991-b4db-c67dfc84a99d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:07:28.976Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2e292628-4528-4565-9eb2-95f5030fa473","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Resources Thread | alternatehistory.com\",\"url\":\"https://www.alternatehistory.com/forum/threads/historical-gis-resources-thread.516971/\"},{\"title\":\"History and Geography of Europe\",\"url\":\"https://euratlas.com/\"},{\"title\":\"And the GIS Data? - Historical Maps>\",\"url\":\"https://www.euratlas.net/history/europe/gis.html\"},{\"title\":\"geoBoundaries\",\"url\":\"https://atlas.co/data-sources/geo-boundaries/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here are several resources available for finding historical European state GIS shapefiles covering pre-1860 Italian duchies and kingdoms:\n\n## Key Resources:\n\n**1. Historical Basemaps Project (GitHub)**\nThis historical boundaries project aims at providing ready-to-use base maps for mapping historical data, with work in progress that should be verified by comparison to other sources before using in academic work.\n\n**2. Census Mosaic / IPUMS**\nCensus Mosaic provides annual information on the administrative division of European states at varying geographic detail across states and time, with data for Prussia (1860-1870) based on geocodes from Patrick Galloway's collection of Prussian statistical data.\n\n**3. Centennia Historical Atlas**\nThe Centennia Historical Atlas has a Research edition available which gives GIS-compatible KML shapefiles for its entire timespan (1000 - 2003).\n\n**4. Euratlas**\nEuratlas provides a historical atlas of Europe with digital vector maps and georeferenced vector maps.\n\n**5. CShapes Dataset**\nCShapes 2.0 is a GIS dataset that maps the borders of states and dependent territories from 1886 through 2019. (Note: This starts after your target pre-1860 period, but earlier versions may have more historical coverage.)\n\n**6. geoBoundaries**\ngeoBoundaries is a free and open-source database of political administrative boundaries that provides high-quality boundary files for countries around the world. However, this primarily focuses on current administrative divisions rather than historical ones.\n\nFor pre-1860 Italian states specifically, the historical-basemaps project and Euratlas appear to be the most promising options for obtaining shapefile data of the duchies and kingdoms that existed before Italian unification.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"35866971-ce09-4748-b68b-d6f0358729a9","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:07:32.120Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"3f4547b2-a2ff-4c69-bdb8-5c83d2dd2cba","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Here are the key findings:\n\n## Euratlas Historical GIS Data\n\nEuratlas maps have been improved and redrawn in 2008 and are available as shapefiles data which can be analyzed and organized spatially with GIS software.\n\n### Data Format and Requirements\nThe data is available in platform-independent shapefile format, and Geographic Information System (GIS) software is required, such as Open Jump, Map Window or Arc GIS.\n\n### Data Layers\nEach century consists of several layers provided in Latin-1 (ISO-8859-1) and UTF-8 encoding: 1) Autonomous peoples, 2) Cities with name and size information, 3) Dioceses/regions as second-level administrative divisions, 4) Sovereign_countries for independent states, and 5) Holder_countries for all entities including dependent territories.\n\nThe search results show that Euratlas offers historical GIS data for multiple time periods (including 1800, which would be close to your 1850 timeframe), with shapefile vector data covering Italian states and other European political entities during pre-unification periods. You would need to visit the Euratlas shop website at euratlas.net to find and download the specific 1850 dataset for Italian states.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"7f4b8ef3-0bb5-4d28-99f4-a30a368af3d2","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:07:51.883Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7ac13a52-676d-4e31-8432-a7bc1a682ef4","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"9175d64b-c8f9-44b7-9a0a-768b0ea00b76","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012QMefQ8BhA6P8QxQBMx1nC","type":"tool_result","content":"# GIS Data 1800 Dataset Overview\n\n**What's Included:**\nThe dataset covers \"the political situation\" of Europe from 15°W to 50°E and 20°N to 60°N. It provides multiple layers including sovereign countries, provinces, cities, dioceses, autonomous peoples, rivers, seas, and mountains with contour lines.\n\n**Italian Pre-Unification States:**\nThe page does not specifically confirm coverage of Italian states like the Papal States, Kingdom of Two Sicilies, Duchy of Modena, Duchy of Parma, Grand Duchy of Tuscany, or Kingdom of Sardinia. However, since the geographic scope encompasses Europe and includes \"sovereign countries\" and provincial divisions for the year 1800, these entities would theoretically be represented, but this isn't explicitly stated.\n\n**Price:**\n\"150 €\" for a simple license (plus VAT), with extended and site license options available.\n\n**Format:**\nShapefiles in \"Latin-1 (ISO-8859-1) and in Unicode Transformation Format 8 (UTF-8)\" formats, requiring GIS software.\n\n**1850 Version:**\nNo 1850 version is mentioned on this page. Available versions range from year 1 through 2000 at centennial intervals (1, 100, 200... 1800, 1900, 2000)."}]},"uuid":"e66fa45f-bc5e-483c-bf72-7dc705cb8c27","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:04.031Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9175d64b-c8f9-44b7-9a0a-768b0ea00b76","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"44fafd95-b558-4838-8c93-5e39c138f73d","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Np2As7d9n8W64qws9QXFV6","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Ceuta Melilla shapefile polygon download GADM Natural Earth Spain\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Download GADM data (version 4.1)\",\"url\":\"https://gadm.org/download_country.html\"},{\"title\":\"Download data by country | DIVA-GIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.diva-gis.org/gdata\"},{\"title\":\"GADM\",\"url\":\"https://www.gadm.org/\"},{\"title\":\"Free GIS Maps of Spain - Resources | Simplemaps.com\",\"url\":\"https://simplemaps.com/gis/country/es\"},{\"title\":\"Natural Earth » 1:10m Cultural Vectors - Free vector and raster map data at 1:10m, 1:50m, and 1:110m scales\",\"url\":\"https://www.naturalearthdata.com/downloads/10m-cultural-vectors/\"},{\"title\":\"Download GADM data (version 3.6)\",\"url\":\"https://gadm.org/download_country_v3.html\"},{\"title\":\"Download free shapefile maps | StatSilk\",\"url\":\"https://www.statsilk.com/maps/download-free-shapefile-maps\"},{\"title\":\"Natural Earth - Free vector and raster map data at 1:10m, 1:50m, and 1:110m scales\",\"url\":\"https://www.naturalearthdata.com/\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - gbif/continents\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/gbif/continents\"},{\"title\":\"Download Shapefiles For Any Country - Mapscaping.com\",\"url\":\"https://mapscaping.com/download-shapefiles-for-any-country/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here are the key resources for downloading Ceuta and Melilla shapefiles:\n\n## Primary Sources:\n\n**GADM (Global Administrative Divisions)**\nGADM provides maps and spatial data for all countries and their sub-divisions. From GADM you can download data in GeoPackage, Shapefile, or GeoJSON formats. You can access GADM at gadm.org to download Spain's administrative boundaries, which includes both Ceuta and Melilla.\n\n**Natural Earth Data**\nNatural Earth may offer KML and additional formats for boundary data and is available at naturalearthdata.com.\n\n**Simplemaps**\nSimplemaps offers downloadable GeoJSON and Shapefile options for Spain, with data that identifies both Ceuta and Melilla as distinct administrative regions.\n\n**DIVA-GIS**\nDIVA-GIS is an alternative source based on GADM, but which also includes roads, railroads, altitude and land cover.\n\n## Download Process:\nNavigate to the GADM website which provides access to administrative boundary data, click on Data Maps at the top, then select Country to choose Spain, and you will see options to download the data in different formats: GeoPackage, Shapefile, or GeoJSON.\n\nAll of these sources include polygon data for both Ceuta and Melilla as Spanish administrative divisions.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"dbd3abaa-9793-4fc0-a27b-2c52e67f93b6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:04.511Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"44fafd95-b558-4838-8c93-5e39c138f73d","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"d7eed41d-a2a8-450c-a22a-3c261a111e16","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014V4HnFZZc72LqGhf2dndFN","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"CShapes 2.0 colonial boundaries combine modern countries approximate historical territories methodology\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"CShapes Dataset of Historical Country Boundaries\",\"url\":\"https://demo.ldproxy.net/cshapes\"},{\"title\":\"Cshapes 2.0 | Library of Congress\",\"url\":\"https://www.loc.gov/item/2023592015/\"},{\"title\":\"The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://d-nb.info/125016981X/34\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - CShapes\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/351339723_Mapping_the_International_System_1886-2019_The_CShapes_20_Dataset\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Research Repository\",\"url\":\"https://repository.essex.ac.uk/31791/\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019 : The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://kops.uni-konstanz.de/entities/publication/31a2030e-4322-4229-93b2-cd6e27e1be8d\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - vLex United States - vLex\",\"url\":\"https://law-journals-books.vlex.com/vid/mapping-the-international-system-879944959\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset | Request PDF\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/342342479_Mapping_the_International_System_1886-2019_The_CShapes_20_Dataset\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I found comprehensive information about CShapes 2.0 and its methodology for handling colonial boundaries and historical territories.\n\n## Overview of CShapes 2.0\n\nCShapes 2.0 is a GIS dataset that maps the borders of states and dependent territories from 1886 through 2019.\n\n## Key Improvements Over Previous Versions\n\nCShapes 2.0 addresses previous limitations by backdating borders to 1886, and by mapping colonial dependencies throughout this entire period. More specifically, it extends temporal coverage from 1946 back to the year 1886, which followed the Berlin Conference on the partition of Africa, and is no longer limited to independent states, but also maps the borders of colonies and other dependencies, thereby providing near complete global coverage of political units throughout recent history.\n\n## Methodology and Data Sources\n\nThe dataset is based on the Gleditsch and Ward (1999) or the Correlates of War coding of independent states, and border changes were coded based on the Territorial Change Dataset by Tir et al (1998), the Encyclopedia of International Boundaries by Biger (1995) and the Encyclopedia of African Boundaries by Brownlie (1979).\n\nThe coding of dependent territories includes four categories from the original COW list: (1) colonies, (2) protectorates, (3) international mandates and (4) occupied territories.\n\n## Temporal Precision\n\nCShapes records the exact date of each territorial change and therefore effectively accounts for changes on a daily basis.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"904587c0-26e1-4df5-b6b8-bc7b0c1cb7a6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:05.739Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d7eed41d-a2a8-450c-a22a-3c261a111e16","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Wake Island\",\"url\":\"https://mapcarta.com/20429046\"},{\"title\":\"How to draw and view boundary data with OpenStreetMap OSM\",\"url\":\"https://blog.afi.io/blog/how-to-draw-and-view-boundary-data-with-openstreetmap-osm/\"},{\"title\":\"Maps of Wake Atoll | Collection of maps of Wake Island | Oceania | Mapsland | Maps of the World\",\"url\":\"https://www.mapsland.com/oceania/wake-atoll\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here are the key findings regarding OpenStreetMap polygon and GeoJSON data for Wake Island and Midway Atoll:\n\n## Key Resources Found:\n\n1. **World GeoJSON Repository**: A GitHub repository (world-geojson) includes both Wake Island and Midway Atoll as part of the USA territories collection. This project provides GeoJSON files for countries and territories.\n\n2. **OpenStreetMap Polygon Service**: The OpenStreetMap polygon service at polygons.openstreetmap.fr allows generating geometries from OSM relation IDs and exporting them as GeoJSON, WKT, or other formats.\n\n3. **Nominatim API**: The Nominatim OpenStreetMap API endpoint supports polygon GeoJSON retrieval using OSM IDs with the polygon_geojson=1 parameter.\n\n4. **General GeoJSON Tools**: Additional resources include osm-countries-geojson (automatically updated countries data from OpenStreetMap) and OSM-Boundaries for extracting administrative boundaries.\n\nTo download specific polygon data for Wake Island and Midway Atoll, you could:\n- Use the world-geojson repository directly from GitHub\n- Query the Nominatim API with the appropriate OSM relation IDs for these atolls\n- Use the OpenStreetMap polygon service if you have the corresponding OSM relation IDs\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"e852be36-ec56-45db-afbc-2a5cac6f7078","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:18.743Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"489a1ca9-3ffd-43e7-bf47-36c5ab0137c6","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

The data consists of 4.5 gigabytes when zip compressed and 18G uncompressed.\n\n## Licensing and Download Options\n\nThe data is available through several licensing options:\n\n- **CRE Complete**: KML data covering 1000AD-2003 with a signed license required, priced at $12,500\n\n- **CRE 1789-1939**: KML data covering the 1789-1939 period available with a license fee of $3125 (signed contract required). This period covers the Italian unification era.\n\n- **Single-Date Option**: KML data for any single year from 1000AD-2003 available for $75 with a signed contract\n\n## Coverage of Italian States\n\nThe Nations Edition covers the 150-year period from 1789 to 1939, including the unifications that led to the creation of modern nation-states of Germany and Italy, which would include detailed mapping of the pre-unification Italian states around 1850.\n\nAll KML datasets are available through the Centennia website (historicalatlas.com) and require contacting them directly for licensing.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"3d39c357-a2da-48b5-baa9-99a2cc67f635","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:19.157Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"446e8d06-c044-4b1a-9d14-ab8b8f3d42f5","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Free vector and raster map data at 1:10m, 1:50m, and 1:110m scales\",\"url\":\"https://www.naturalearthdata.com/downloads/10m-cultural-vectors/10m-admin-0-breakaway-disputed-areas/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's information about Natural Earth's 10m admin 0 map data:\n\n## Coverage and Organization\n\nNatural Earth includes 209 sovereign states, with auxiliary themes comprising 298 cartographic map units and 360 subunits optimized for labeling edge cases, such as the four constituent countries of England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland in the United Kingdom.\n\n## Territories and Small Principalities\n\nThe data covers territories including Antarctica, some disputed areas, tiny island nations, and principalities such as Monaco, Andorra, Liechtenstein, and San Marino.\n\n## Data Structure and Attributes\n\nMap subunits are optimized for labeling edge cases, and the data is organized into different categories. The data includes:\n\n- **Boundary Features**: Land boundaries and 200-mile nautical limits, with limited attributes in terms of type of boundary: disputed, treaty, and median lines\n\n- **Boundary Representation**: Natural Earth shows de facto boundaries by default according to who controls the territory, versus de jure, and can be adjusted using fclass_* properties to create different point-of-view worldviews.\n\nThe search results indicate that Natural Earth provides comprehensive coverage of administrative boundaries at the 10m scale, including small territories and principalities, with various attributes to support different mapping and cartographic needs.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"b2311aa9-c1de-4049-ba87-61b9068c79e6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:30.632Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2d9f0bca-0115-4575-ad87-5233332c6643","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Here's what the search revealed about these pre-1886 historical states:\n\n## Kingdom of the Two Sicilies\n\nThe Kingdom of the Two Sicilies was a kingdom in Southern Italy from 1816 to 1861 under the control of the House of Bourbon-Two Sicilies, a cadet branch of the Bourbons. The kingdom was the largest sovereign state by population and land area in Italy before the Italian unification, comprising Sicily and most of the area of today's Mezzogiorno (southern Italy) and covering all of the Italian peninsula south of the Papal States.\n\n## Papal States\n\nThe Papal States were a conglomeration of territories on the Italian peninsula under the direct sovereign rule of the pope from 756 to 1870. {"parentUuid":"7777d9b3-d4b3-4ba9-9674-27de5ce0464c","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01YYtPY1WzFiLnDESDeJNU4h","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"CShapes 2.0 R package cshapes list all entities colonial territories French West Africa Indochina\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - CShapes\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - R Discovery\",\"url\":\"https://discovery.researcher.life/article/mapping-the-international-system-1886-2019-the-cshapes-2-0-dataset/fe9ded6b9fff361783efa982604cc051\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping and Measuring Country Shapes\",\"url\":\"https://journal.r-project.org/articles/RJ-2010-004/index.html\"},{\"title\":\"The Geography of the International System: The CShapes Dataset: International Interactions: Vol 36, No 1\",\"url\":\"https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/03050620903554614\"},{\"title\":\"Indochina – PPSC HIS 1120: The World: 1500-Present\",\"url\":\"https://pressbooks.ccconline.org/the-world-1500-present/chapter/indochina/\"},{\"title\":\"Chapter 5.3 Colonial Empires – Western Civilization II\",\"url\":\"https://louis.pressbooks.pub/westernciv2/chapter/5-3colonial-empires/\"},{\"title\":\"The Heart of the Matter French West Africa and British West Africa | GradeSaver\",\"url\":\"https://www.gradesaver.com/the-heart-of-the-matter/study-guide/french-west-africa-and-british-west-africa\"},{\"title\":\"Living Beyond Boundaries: West African Servicemen in French Colonial Conflicts, 1908-1962\",\"url\":\"https://escholarship.org/uc/item/4x19q2xb\"},{\"title\":\"The end of empire in French West Africa : France's successful decolonization? : Chafer, Tony : Free Download, Borrow, and Streaming : Internet Archive\",\"url\":\"https://archive.org/details/endofempireinfre0000chaf\"}]\n\nI've completed a web search on your query about CShapes 2.0 R package and colonial territories in French West Africa and Indochina. Here are the key findings:\n\n## CShapes 2.0 Dataset Overview\n\nCShapes 2.0 is a GIS dataset that maps the borders of states and dependent territories from 1886 through 2019. The new dataset is no longer limited to independent states, but also maps the borders of colonies and other dependencies, thereby providing near complete global coverage of political units throughout recent history.\n\n## Coverage of Colonial Territories\n\nThe dataset can be downloaded in various formats and is also accessible through the CShapes R package. This means you can access information about colonial entities including French West Africa and Indochina through the R package interface.\n\n## French Colonial Territories Mentioned\n\nThe search results provide information about the colonial entities you're interested in:\n\n**French West Africa:** French West Africa was a federation of eight French colonial territories that existed from 1895 to 1960, including Mauritania, Senegal, French Sudan (Mali), French Guinea, Cote d'Ivoire, Upper Volta (Burkina Faso), Dahomey (Benin), and Niger.\n\n**French Indochina:** French Indochina was a group of French colonial territories in Southeast Asia, with Vietnamese regions of Tonkin, Annam, and Cochinchina combined with Cambodia in 1887.\n\nFor detailed instructions on listing all entities within the CShapes 2.0 R package specifically, you may want to consult the package documentation directly or the CShapes project website.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"11a4cbef-aaf6-4891-a2b9-a312afeade0c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:09:02.614Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7777d9b3-d4b3-4ba9-9674-27de5ce0464c","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Humanities GIS - InfoGuides at George Mason University\",\"url\":\"https://infoguides.gmu.edu/geohumanities/data\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about historical basemaps, ThinkQuest, and related resources for the 1850-1860 period:\n\n## Historical-Basemaps Project\n\nThe historical-basemaps project is a collection of basemaps collected, adapted and converted from diverse sources, sometimes only available through the wayback machine, with among these sources being anonymous students from the \"ThinkQuest Team C006628\". These maps are available in GeoJSON format.\n\n## ThinkQuest Data\n\nThe ThinkQuest.org site consolidated a series of country boundary data into shapefiles, created as an educational site in 1996 and acquired by Oracle in 2002, but went defunct in 2013; thanks to the Wayback Machine, the shapefiles of country boundaries spanning between 2000 BCE and 1994 CE can still be downloaded.\n\nHowever, the data should be used with caution and only for small scale projects, as it was created by students and has a spatial error of roughly +/- 40 miles, especially for the oldest years.\n\n## Regarding Italian States and Papal States\n\nThe search results confirm that the 1850-1860 period was historically significant for Italian territories. In 1860, with much of the region already in rebellion against Papal rule, Piedmont-Sardinia invaded and conquered the eastern two-thirds of the Papal States.\n\nBoth the historical-basemaps project and the archived ThinkQuest data should contain boundary information for this period, though the quality caveats noted above should be considered when using them.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"9ba21709-7c73-472d-aadd-ec222919b81c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:09:03.256Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"3f64926a-8707-479a-b507-2c43597bf539","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

historical GIS resources:\n\nThe MPIDR Population History GIS Collection supports demographic and socioeconomic research by filling gaps in the European GIS-data-infrastructure on historical national annual information on the administrative division at varying geographic detail across states and time, covering all states that became part of the German Empire in 1871, with geocodes for Prussia {"parentUuid":"fc9313af-fd22-4c36-b82d-11eb67534268","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XUcFdhigD5zaYMzath671C","type":"tool_result","content":"# CShapes Dataset Overview\n\n## Available Features\n\nThe webpage doesn't enumerate specific entities or colonial territories. It only states the dataset \"maps the borders and capitals of independent states and dependent territories from 1886 to 2019.\"\n\n## Colonial Territories\n\nThe page provides no information about which specific colonial territories (French Indochina, British Malaya, Belgian Congo, etc.) are included in the dataset.\n\n## How to Query\n\nThe page offers several access methods:\n\n1. **Download formats**: GeoJSON, shapefiles, CSV, FlatGeobuf, and JSON-FG files\n2. **API access**: Through the OGC API Standards endpoint at https://demo.ldproxy.net/cshapes\n3. **Predefined queries**: Available at https://demo.ldproxy.net/cshapes/search\n4. **Direct data access**: Navigate to https://demo.ldproxy.net/cshapes/collections for the boundary data\n\nFor detailed query specifications, consult the formal API documentation or contact the technical support contact listed (Clemens Portele at interactive instruments GmbH)."}]},"uuid":"6f596f5b-1712-436d-a251-17aee733df9a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:09:25.407Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"fc9313af-fd22-4c36-b82d-11eb67534268","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"f254ec92-ea89-400f-8d00-7eb1d56ed943","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0139r7XueGTY5RaJBQ7QgAQF","type":"tool_result","content":"# Dataset Coverage Analysis\n\nBased on the repository documentation, here's what I can tell you:\n\n**Available Years:**\nThe dataset includes snapshots from: \"2000BC, 500BC, 323BC, 200BC, 1BC, 400, 600, 800, 1000, 1279, 1492, 1530, 1650, 1715, 1783, 1880, 1914, 1920\" plus a world basemap. There is **no data for 1850 or 1860**.\n\n**Italian Pre-unification States:**\nThe documentation does not specify which territories or historical political entities are represented in individual time periods.\n\n**File Format:**\nThe data is provided as a **TopoJSON** file. The package exports functions to access collections as GeoJSON FeatureCollections, suggesting conversion capabilities are included.\n\n**Data Quality Note:**\nThe source material carries significant limitations: \"created by students, the archived disclaimer page explains that the data has a spatial error of roughly +/- 40 miles\" and should be used primarily for small-scale visualization projects.\n\nFor detailed information about specific territorial coverage, you would need to examine the actual TopoJSON file or contact the repository maintainer directly."}]},"uuid":"d7f70065-be85-4217-ac13-d3001460806b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:09:26.638Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f254ec92-ea89-400f-8d00-7eb1d56ed943","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Let me provide what I found:\n\nCShapes 2.0 maps the borders and capitals of independent states and dependent territories from 1886 to 2019. Dependent territories are units that are under the control of an independent state, but that are not considered part of its core territory, typically non-adjacent territories that are ruled as colonies or protectorates.\n\nYou can download the latest version (2.0) of the raw CShapes data directly in CSV (UTF-8 encoded), GeoJSON, Shapefile, SQL, and R package formats from the ETH International Conflict Research website at icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/.\n\nHowever, the search results do not provide the specific total count of how many dependent territories or colonies are included in the dataset. Map of Papal States in Year 1400\",\"url\":\"https://www.euratlas.net/history/europe/1400/entity_2140.html\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I found relevant information about Euratlas Periodis historical GIS shapefiles for the 1800 and 1900 periods:\n\n## Euratlas Periodis Historical GIS Shapefiles\n\nThe Euratlas maps have been improved and redrawn in 2008 and are available as shapefiles data which can be analyzed and organized spatially with a GIS software.\n\n### Data Availability for 1800 and 1900\n\nThe data is provided in platform-independent shapefile format. Each century consists of several layers including autonomous peoples, cities with name and size information, dioceses/regions, sovereign countries, and holder countries.\n\n### Pricing\n\nRegarding your question about free access, these GIS data sets are now available for sale, especially to educational or research institutions, publishers or museums. The shapefiles are not free. The page includes a visual representation labeled \"Map of Europe 1800 showing the location of Papal States,\" though the actual detailed map image requires membership access for larger versions.\n\n## Boundaries and Display\n\nThe page notes that \"This map is in Sovereign States mode, zoom in from the main map to display the dependencies,\" suggesting boundary information exists but requires interaction with the full map interface.\n\n## Other Italian States Listed\n\nSeveral Italian territories appear in the comprehensive entity list for 1800:\n\n- **Naples** (marked as Sovereign state)\n- **Parma** (Sovereign state)\n- **Tuscany** (Dependent territory)\n- **Genoa** (Dependent territory)\n- **Sardinia** (Sovereign state)\n- **Malta** (Dependent territory)\n- **Ragusa** (Dependent territory)\n- **San Marino** (Sovereign state)\n\nThe Papal States themselves are noted as a \"Dependent\" entity, specifically dependent on Naples during this period."}]},"uuid":"cc907ac1-f2b3-4f04-88d3-35f160411243","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:28.194Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"582867b3-f7c5-43f1-bca8-b752b52e6447","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

aourednik/historical-basemaps: Collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about OSHistory's histgeodata and related historical GIS resources for European boundaries and pre-unification Italian states:\n\n## OSHistory/histgeodata Repository\n\nOSHistory/histgeodata is a collection of Historical GIS files mainly from Europe, Germany and Austro-Hungarian Empire. This repository is a list of links to historical vector-based GIS data.\n\n## Historical GIS Resources for European Boundaries\n\nThe search revealed several important resources for historical European boundaries:\n\nThere are international boundaries available for Italy from 1815-1866, which covers the pre-unification period. The MPIDR Population History GIS Collection supports demographic and socioeconomic research by filling gaps in the European GIS-data-infrastructure on historical national and regional administrative boundaries and historical place names.\n\n## Pre-Unification Italian States\n\nResearch covers four pre-unification Italian states: the Sabaudian State, Florentine State, Kingdom of Naples, and Republic of Venice, during the period 1300–1800. Northern Italy was made up of states including the Kingdom of Lombardy-Venetia, which included Venice and Milan, and the Kingdom of Sardinia, which included Piedmont, Savoy and Nice.\n\nEuratlas History Maps is a historical atlas of Europe from year 1 to present days with one map per century, depicting sovereign states as well as administrative divisions and dependent territories.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"e8d29683-ca0c-44c6-807a-5b52c9549727","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:33.463Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"da16c0e7-b655-48c2-ab88-cef1b26d84e8","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

CShapes 2017 dataset - historical maps of state boundaries and capitals\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/shapefile.html\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about CShapes Europe and its coverage:\n\n## CShapes Europe Dataset Overview\n\nCShapes 2.0 maps the borders and capitals of independent states and dependent territories from 1886 to 2019 and from 1816 for CShapes-Europe.\n\n## European Extension (1816 pre-1886)\n\nFor the European extension covering the 1816-1886 period, the data were extended for Europe back to 1816 drawing on non-spatial data from the Gleditsch and Ward (1999) dataset of independent states, the Correlates of War's Territorial Change dataset, and the Centennia Historical Atlas, with the addition of dozens of microstates that existed before the German and Italian unifications.\n\n## Italian States Coverage\n\nThe dataset specifically includes coverage of Italian territories during this pre-unification period. Geo-Larhra provides international boundaries in Italy from 1815-1866, which overlaps with the CShapes-Europe period you're interested in.\n\n## Download Formats\n\nCShapes data is available directly in CSV (UTF-8 encoded), GeoJSON, Shapefile, SQL, and R package formats. The CShapes GIS dataset is provided in shapefile format, which can be processed by most GIS packages.\n\nYou can download the dataset from the official ETH International Conflict Research page at https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"fdb57bfc-b352-4b8d-b0a2-30554f13c81b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:33.597Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"6d1c18fc-14a0-48b7-8055-38c401e0572a","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"ac3fcc3d-3952-4774-aaf8-905553b47201","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac699526b8bf7af7d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01W7g8FrNGD7o3qYrTLveipR","type":"tool_result","content":"# Historical GIS Datasets and European Boundaries\n\n## All Datasets Listed\n\n**Cross-Country Resources:**\n- CensusMosaic, Cshapes, European Borders 1938-1944, Geo-Larhra, Histo-GIS\n\n**Country-Specific:**\n- China, Great Britain, Germany, Netherlands, Low-Countries, Japan, USA\n\n## European Boundaries Data\n\nThe repository highlights several European options:\n\n**Cshapes** provides \"changing world borders (1886 to present)\" with both download and interactive web access options.\n\n**Histo-GIS** focuses on Central Europe, offering \"historical vector data for germany and austria\" with emphasis on the Austro-Hungarian Empire territories.\n\n**Geo-Larhra** covers \"france, benelux and italy\" as shapefile data.\n\n## Italian Pre-Unification States\n\nThe repository does not explicitly mention pre-1860s Italian states or Geo-Larhra's specific temporal coverage for Italy (1815-1866). While Geo-Larhra includes Italy in its geographic scope, the README provides no details about whether it covers the fragmented peninsula before unification.
